role: Tech Lead — Cloud & Data Platform Engineering
eyebrow: AWS · GitOps · Data Engineering · Infrastructure Automation
headline: Enabling a cloud data platform that gave a global benefits provider reliable, automated infrastructure for its data workloads across 4 enterprise environments.
context: Alight Solutions is a global benefits administration provider. The engagement required building a reliable, automated cloud data platform on AWS to support enterprise data workloads across multiple product teams.
challenge: The platform needed standardized IaC, secure CI/CD pipelines, and scalable data engineering infrastructure — all while enforcing security across every layer of delivery across 4 enterprise environments.
approach: Led a 7-member cloud and data engineering team as hands-on tech lead. Designed and built reusable Terraform modules for VPC endpoints, compute, and storage provisioning, standardizing infrastructure delivery across 10 product teams. Configured GitHub Actions CI/CD pipelines on GitOps principles — automating build, test, and deploy lifecycles from commit to production across 4 enterprise environments. Delivered data engineering infrastructure using AWS Glue, Redshift, Step Functions, and SageMaker. Integrated AWS Bedrock for AI-assisted platform capabilities. Enforced end-to-end security through IAM policies, VPC endpoint configurations, and CodeDeploy-managed release controls.
architecture: Modular Terraform codebase with environment-specific variable files, standardizing provisioning across 10 product teams. GitHub Actions pipelines built on GitOps principles — commit-triggered builds flowing through test and deploy stages across 4 enterprise environments. Redshift clusters provisioned behind private subnets with IAM role-based access. Glue jobs orchestrated via Step Functions with S3 as the data lake layer. CodeDeploy managing release controls for consistent, auditable deployments.
owned: Technical leadership of a 7-member cloud and data engineering team
Reusable Terraform module design for VPC endpoints, compute, and storage — standardizing delivery across 10 product teams
GitHub Actions CI/CD pipelines on GitOps principles across 4 enterprise environments
Data engineering infrastructure: AWS Glue, Redshift, Step Functions, SageMaker
AWS Bedrock integration for AI-assisted platform capabilities
End-to-end security enforcement via IAM policies, VPC endpoints, and CodeDeploy release controls
outcomes: Reusable Terraform modules reduced provisioning time for new data workloads across 10 product teams
GitOps pipelines standardized delivery across 4 enterprise environments, reducing configuration drift
CodeDeploy-managed releases improved deployment consistency and auditability
Data engineering team gained reliable, self-service infrastructure for pipeline development
AWS Bedrock integration enabled AI-assisted capabilities on the platform
lessons: Reusability in IaC pays off fastest when the team agrees on module boundaries early. GitOps principles applied to infrastructure — not just application code — give you the audit trail and rollback confidence that enterprise environments demand.

role: Junior Tech Lead — Multi-Cloud DevOps & Platform Engineering
eyebrow: Azure · AWS · GCP · Multi-Cloud Modernisation · Client Delivery
headline: Accelerating cloud modernisation for global enterprise clients across Azure, AWS, and GCP — from landing zones and security remediation to production-grade CI/CD pipelines.
context: Multi-client engagement covering cloud migration, security remediation, and platform modernization across Azure, AWS, and GCP for four enterprise clients across retail, manufacturing, telecoms, and agriculture sectors.
challenge: Each client had different starting points — legacy on-premise workloads, inconsistent deployment practices, security debt, and limited cloud governance. The challenge was to deliver cloud migration and modernisation at enterprise scale while maintaining security, compliance, and operational consistency across three cloud providers.
approach: Worked as a DevOps engineer and lead within IBM's Azure Cloud Practice across four concurrent client engagements. For H&M: drove cloud migration and security remediation for 100+ applications, enforcing tiered SLA remediation (High: 8 hrs, Medium: 48 hrs, Low: 7 days) via SonarQube and Coverity; provisioned Azure landing zones via Terraform; defined migration approach — lift-and-shift, modernization, and infrastructure upscale — alongside app owners; executed SQL-based data migration and migrated application code to GitHub. For Siemens: provisioned AWS Terraform landing zone and GitHub Actions pipelines; released containerized applications into AKS namespaces via Helm and ACR; secured all deployment workflows with OIDC authentication. For Südzucker: provisioned GCP landing zone via Terraform and GitHub Actions. For Nokia: supported Azure migration for a US government-aligned client under active security clearance (clearance expired 2024); delivered compliance-driven infrastructure under regulated enterprise requirements.
architecture: Multi-cloud delivery using Azure landing zones as the governance foundation. H&M: Terraform-provisioned Azure infrastructure with private endpoints, firewall rules, and SonarQube/Coverity security scanning integrated into GitHub Actions pipelines. Siemens: AWS Terraform landing zone with containerized workloads deployed into AKS namespaces via Helm and ACR, OIDC-secured pipelines. Südzucker: GCP landing zone via Terraform and GitHub Actions. Nokia: compliance-driven Azure infrastructure under regulated enterprise requirements.
owned: H&M: cloud migration and security remediation for 100+ applications — SonarQube, Coverity, tiered SLA enforcement
H&M: Azure landing zone provisioning via Terraform; migration approach definition across 100+ apps
H&M: SQL-based data migration and application code migration to GitHub
Siemens: AWS Terraform landing zone, AKS/Helm deployments, OIDC-secured GitHub Actions pipelines
Südzucker: GCP landing zone provisioning via Terraform and GitHub Actions
Nokia: compliance-driven Azure infrastructure delivery under active security clearance
Multi-cloud Terraform provisioning across Azure, AWS, and GCP
outcomes: Delivered cloud migration and modernisation programs for H&M, Siemens, Nokia, and Südzucker
100+ H&M applications assessed and migrated with security remediation enforced via tiered SLA
Established repeatable landing zone and governance patterns adopted across client engagements
OIDC-secured Siemens pipelines eliminated credential-based deployment risks
Enabled client teams to operate cloud workloads independently post-migration
lessons: Enterprise cloud migrations succeed when governance is designed in from day one, not retrofitted. Security remediation at scale requires process discipline — tiered SLAs turn a backlog into a managed programme.

role: Software Engineer — Cloud Adoption & Platform Enablement
eyebrow: Azure · AWS · Terraform · Cloud Adoption · DevOps
headline: Delivering cloud infrastructure automation, migration, and DevOps pipelines across Azure and AWS for clients in pharmaceutical research, reinsurance, and utilities.
context: Multi-client engagement delivering cloud infrastructure automation, migration, and DevOps pipelines across Azure and AWS for clients in pharmaceutical research, reinsurance, and utilities.
challenge: Each client needed reliable, automated cloud delivery — from IaC-driven Azure provisioning and YAML pipeline management, to lift-and-shift migrations and AWS operational support — with security and compliance embedded throughout.
approach: For Parexel: delivered Azure IaC automation using Terraform, ARM Templates, Bicep, and Ansible; managed Azure DevOps YAML pipelines and self-hosted VM environments. For GenRe: executed Azure lift-and-shift migration using ARM Templates and Blueprint-based landing zones; performed data transfer via Azure Direct Connect. For Veolia Water: supported AWS environment with CloudWatch monitoring and IAM access governance; managed EC2 and S3 deployments.
architecture: Parexel: Terraform, ARM Templates, and Bicep for Azure IaC; Azure DevOps YAML pipelines with self-hosted VM agents. GenRe: ARM Template and Blueprint-based Azure landing zones for lift-and-shift migration; Azure Direct Connect for data transfer. Veolia Water: AWS EC2 and S3 deployments with CloudWatch monitoring and IAM governance.
owned: Parexel: Azure IaC automation — Terraform, ARM Templates, Bicep, Ansible
Parexel: Azure DevOps YAML pipeline management and self-hosted VM environments
GenRe: Azure lift-and-shift migration via ARM Templates and Blueprint-based landing zones
GenRe: data transfer via Azure Direct Connect
Veolia Water: AWS EC2 and S3 deployments with CloudWatch monitoring and IAM governance
outcomes: Delivered IaC-automated Azure infrastructure for Parexel, reducing manual provisioning overhead
Executed GenRe lift-and-shift migration to Azure with Blueprint-based governance
Veolia Water AWS environment stabilised with CloudWatch monitoring and IAM access controls
Contributed to growing the cloud practice's delivery capacity across three client verticals
lessons: Lift-and-shift migrations succeed when landing zone governance is established before the first workload moves. IaC consistency across ARM, Bicep, and Terraform gives teams flexibility without sacrificing standardisation.

headline: Building maintainable Android applications with clean architecture patterns at a product-focused software company.
context: Early-career role at a Mumbai-based software product and services company. This was the foundation of a professional engineering career — learning to write production code, work within a team, and build software that real users depend on.
challenge: Delivering Android application features that were maintainable, testable, and consistent with the team's architecture standards across multiple client projects.
approach: Developed Android applications using MVVM architecture patterns across multiple client projects. Built and maintained UI components with a focus on code readability and maintainability. Worked within a team codebase, following code review processes and engineering standards.
architecture: MVVM pattern separating UI, business logic, and data layers. Android UI components built with XML layouts and data binding. Version control via Git with team-based branching and code review.
owned: Android application feature development across multiple client projects
MVVM architecture implementation for UI components
Code quality and maintainability within team standards
Participation in code review and engineering processes
outcomes: Delivered Android application features that shipped to production across client projects
Developed foundational engineering habits — clean code, architecture discipline, team collaboration
Built the technical foundation that enabled rapid growth into cloud and platform engineering roles
lessons: The habits formed in early engineering roles — clean architecture, code review discipline, and thinking about maintainability — compound over time. This is where the foundation was built.
